title: dactylorhiza maculata subsp. okellyi
intro: Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. okellyi, commonly known as the spotted marsh orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to Ireland. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. okellyi include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. okellyi is typically prepared as a tincture or infused in herbal teas, and it is also used in traditional remedies for its antiseptic properties, particularly for treating skin conditions and wounds.
